items: Quiet update notice replaces intrusive first-viewport update toast.
Actual 460-item look/code review is wired into the in-app improvement drawer.
Sequential workspace smoke command added to avoid visual-smoke browser contention.
Visual smoke manifest now includes screenshot labels.
Shared route error and loading components added.
Typed localStorage helper added with schema validation and corrupted-entry recovery.
Route-to-owner registry added with dependencies, smoke text, and escalation path.
Route registry is included in diagnostic bundles.
Improvement drawer shows owner, dependencies, smoke text, and escalation path.
Hidden page titles added from the shell for routes that lacked H1 coverage.
Command palette has a first-class Actions group for task, note, draft, and agent-job workflows.
Command palette quick actions have desktop keyboard shortcuts with editable-field guards.
Shared source freshness formatter added for Dashboard and Phone Cockpit.
Visual smoke supports route-specific visible selector assertions.
Command palette includes direct actions for common Settings sections.
Command palette now exposes meeting prep, Barry check-ins, waiting/delegated/blocked task reviews, MCP setup, profile routing, ConnectWise queue, and Swarm control actions.
Command palette now includes the Swarm Control Plane route in screen search, separate from the legacy Swarm route.
Desktop command palette shortcuts now include meeting prep, Barry check-ins, and Swarm controls with editable-field guards.
Shared source freshness utility now owns cross-page status tone normalization, tone ranking, and light/dark chip classes.
PageStatusStrip now uses the shared workspace status classes instead of a private tone map.
Ops Intelligence and Workspace Health now derive status chip classes from shared workspace tone normalization.
IT Ops freshness copy and action/source chips now use shared workspace freshness and status tone helpers.
Improvement drawer completion persistence now uses the typed storage helper with boolean-record validation.
Page diagnostics persistence now uses the typed storage helper with diagnostic-event array validation and corrupted-entry recovery.
Phone Cockpit now has persisted Standard, Driving, and Walking daily modes.
Phone Cockpit driving and walking modes render an ultra-compact daily glance focused on urgent count, next event, capture, and LILY mic.
Tasks now has a mobile-only compact glance with today's promise, overdue/waiting/delegated counts, quick filter chips, and next-task shortcuts.
MCP now has a guided setup strip that derives config source, connection health, tool discovery, and auth-review steps from the live server list.
MCP guided setup includes a direct registry-browse action while keeping the live capability matrix and skill handoff routes in the same page flow.
New-user workflow audit confirmed meeting prep, task handoffs, profile diff previews, ConnectWise deep links, and swarm worker controls are present and covered by route helpers/tests.
Shared page diagnostics now normalize /workspace routes before matching route registry metadata.
Shared page diagnostics now include route context with desktop/mobile smoke assertions and route smoke fixtures.
Shared page diagnostics now include live source drilldown targets derived from each route's runtime dependencies.
Visible-page recommendation catalog now covers Wegovy Shots, Zyn Tracker, and Food Log as first-class daily pages.
Visible-page catalog tests now guard all smoke-test routes against missing recommendation coverage.
Route diagnostic context centralizes fixture expectations for Jobs, Tasks, 75 Tracker, and Operations active-state checks.
Gateway mission checkpoint and mission history persistence now use typed storage validation with malformed-entry recovery.
Gateway approvals persistence now uses typed storage validation while preserving resolved-approval cleanup.
Gateway custom workflow templates now use typed storage validation instead of ad hoc JSON parsing.
Jobs saved filter persistence now uses typed JSON storage with legacy raw-string migration.
Gateway persistence store tests now cover malformed localStorage recovery for mission, approval, and workflow template state.
Chat message rendering now re-enables bounded virtualization for long non-search timelines while preserving pinned last-exchange behavior.
Live agent stream panels now render a bounded latest-message window and point older messages to the full chat.
Memory search now makes its first-20 result window explicit with a narrow-query hint for hidden matches.
Performance pass keeps search and pinned states unvirtualized to avoid scroll glitches while protecting long timelines.
Visual smoke retries transient runtime navigation and stale-asset recovery redirects.
Smoke commands preflight LaunchAgent/client asset synchronization before route and visual checks.
Route smoke retries transient navigation timeouts with a fresh page before failing a route.
Route smoke auth now backs off on local 429 rate limits instead of running unauthenticated page checks.
Update notice no longer covers the dashboard header.
Gateway stale state promoted to a visible incident/recovery banner.
Visible Dashboard action queue heading added.
Stale overview copy now points to live-evidence refresh before acting.
Dashboard freshness and stale checks now use the shared workspace freshness utility.
Dashboard now computes a single ranked next action from blockers, stale sources, or the current workstream.
Dashboard action queue now has a compact command strip for running the next action.
Dashboard command strip now exposes hidden-widget recovery without requiring users to discover layout edit mode.
Dashboard command strip can copy a safe diagnostics bundle with health, stale state, freshness, hidden widgets, degraded sources, and severity map.
Dashboard action cards now include inline severity chips for faster scanning.
Dashboard diagnostics explicitly exclude secrets and tokens.
Dashboard command-center scoring, status brief, next-action ranking, diagnostics, and workflow presets now live in a route-private pure utility module.
Dashboard command strip now lives in a route-private component module instead of the main route file.
Dashboard helper tests now import pure command-center logic directly instead of importing the full route component.
Dashboard route file is reduced from 2876 to 2633 lines while preserving the current command-center behavior.
Dashboard shared palette, card shell, unavailable widget, time/number formatting, color alpha, and secondary action primitives now live in a route-private UI module.
Dashboard route file is reduced from 2633 to 2444 lines while preserving current first-viewport, command strip, session, and widget behavior.
Dashboard UI primitive extraction keeps the shared source-freshness footer and icon-button semantics consistent across existing route cards.
Dashboard action rail, daily signals, weekly utilization, control plane, LILY readiness, and daily flow rail now live in a route-private section module.
Dashboard route file is reduced from 2443 to 1495 lines while preserving API ownership, command-center state, widget layout, charts, and navigation behavior.
Dashboard section extraction keeps route-owned data loading, action ranking, and navigation callbacks in the screen while moving display-only dashboard surfaces out of the route file.
Update notice no longer covers the urgent-task headline.
Duplicate hidden H1 removed.
Loading state no longer becomes the H1 text.
Additional bottom safe-area spacing protects pinned actions from bottom navigation.
Queued captures now use typed storage validation.
Collapsed card preferences now use typed storage validation.
Pinned card preferences now use typed storage validation.
Pin and collapse controls are icon-only touch targets with accessible labels.
Phone freshness labels now use the shared workspace freshness utility.
Phone tabs, card preference helpers, mode readouts, at-a-glance summaries, signal rail, daily loops, quick logs, freshness notices, and queue readouts now live in a route-private helper module.
Phone helper tests import the pure helper module directly instead of the full route component.
Phone route file is reduced from 3017 to 2335 lines while preserving current daily-mode behavior.
Phone shared status dot, action link, icon tile, card, empty state, external action, and stat pill primitives now live in a route-private UI module.
Phone route imports UI primitives directly instead of carrying duplicate JSX helper definitions.
Phone route file is reduced from 2335 to 2113 lines while preserving current daily-mode behavior.

Phone top glance, view controls, fast actions, daily loops, desk mode, system status/source health drawer, workspace shortcuts, floating LILY action, and sticky quick note now live in a route-private sections module.

Phone sections extraction keeps the route-owned state machine in the screen while moving display-only mobile surfaces out of the route file.
Chat receives a hidden route-level H1 from the shell.
Update notice no longer competes with the chat status strip.
Chat workflow panel now has a first-run command rail for task capture, knowledge note, draft reply, and agent-job starts.

Chat diagnostics and command rail helpers have focused coverage for safe export, resume context, and first-run visibility.
Chat workflow controls keep command shortcuts in the workflow panel instead of adding more inline main-surface guidance.
Chat timeline classification, flow-risk detection, workflow summary, route diagnostics, resume-latest context, and command rail now live in a route-private workflow module.
Chat helper tests now import pure workflow logic directly instead of loading the full chat route for summary coverage.
Chat route file is reduced from 3504 to 3215 lines while preserving current workflow panel, diagnostics, and first-run command behavior.

Chat portable-history, export, retry-key, attachment, timestamp, optimistic-message, duplicate-collapse, and queued-wrapper helpers now live in a route-private helper module.
Chat route file is reduced from 3215 to 2677 lines while preserving session orchestration, streaming, retries, composer behavior, and route smoke behavior.
Chat public route entrypoint now delegates to a route-private implementation module so the imported screen surface stays tiny while preserving existing lazy route imports.

HermesWorld receives a hidden route-level H1 from the shell.

Playground route imports the title surface directly so the main route can focus on gameplay state, overlays, and world orchestration.
Playground overlay, modal, low-health, camera-toast, loading, help HUD, right rail, mobile ability, nearby builder, and utility dock surfaces now live in a route-private component module.
